mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain flat or negative when UNRATE crosses below 8.0 in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding industrial production signal synchronized real-sector recovery, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, supply constraints captured by industrial production become the primary driver of corporate margins, meaning rising output directly boosts equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO produces negative or zero SPX forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225 in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real consumer credit reflects durable demand, which mechanically supports forward corporate revenue and equity prices over short horizons.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25 percent for consecutive months, liquidity conditions remain accommodative, sustaining equity risk premiums and supporting positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for three months in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising employment and stagnant unemployment indicates structural labor market healing, which historically triggers positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint movement of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating GDP growth, corporate earnings momentum accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns do not materialize following quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% in more than 50% of observations.
